strategy.name: DaviddTech NNFX Trend System
strategy.description: NNFX-based trend following system. Baseline sets direction, MACD confirms momentum, ADX filters out choppy markets, ATR bands define exits. All components must align. Never trade a single indicator alone.

indicators.ema_8: Baseline — fast trend direction. Price above EMA(8) = bullish momentum. Below = bearish. Acts as the NNFX baseline equivalent.
indicators.ema_21: Slow trend confirmation. Price above EMA(21) = sustained uptrend. Both EMAs must align for a valid bias.
indicators.macd: Entry confirmation — MACD line crossing above signal = bullish momentum confirmed. Crossing below = bearish. Zero-lag version preferred per DaviddTech.
indicators.rsi_14: Market regime filter. RSI between 40-60 = choppy/ranging market — avoid entries. Above 60 = trending up, below 40 = trending down.
indicators.vwap: Session bias. Price above VWAP = institutional buyers in control. Below = sellers. Resets at midnight UTC.
indicators.adx: Choppiness filter — DaviddTech's most consistent rule: ADX below 25 = ranging market, skip all trades. ADX above 25 = trending, allow entries.
indicators.atr: Exit engine — stop loss at 1.5x ATR below entry (longs) or above entry (shorts). Take profit at 2x ATR. Adapts to current volatility.

neutral: EMA(8) and EMA(21) are within 0.1% of each other — no clear trend
Price is between EMA(8) and EMA(21) — transition zone, avoid

long: Market bias is bullish — price above both EMA(8) and EMA(21) and VWAP
ADX is above 25 — market is trending, not choppy
MACD line has crossed above the signal line — momentum confirmed bullish
MACD histogram is positive and expanding — momentum is accelerating, not fading
RSI(14) is above 50 but below 70 — trending up without being overbought
Price is within 2% of VWAP — not overextended from session fair value
short: Market bias is bearish — price below both EMA(8) and EMA(21) and VWAP
ADX is above 25 — market is trending, not choppy
MACD line has crossed below the signal line — momentum confirmed bearish
MACD histogram is negative and expanding — momentum is accelerating downward
RSI(14) is below 50 but above 30 — trending down without being oversold
Price is within 2% of VWAP — not overextended from session fair value

exit_rules: Stop loss: 1.5x ATR from entry — hard stop, no exceptions
Take profit: 2x ATR from entry — minimum 1.33 reward-to-risk ratio
Exit immediately if MACD crosses back against trade direction
Exit if ADX drops below 20 — trend is dying, protect gains
Trail stop to breakeven once trade reaches 1x ATR profit
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1% of portfolio per trade — position size calculated from ATR stop distance
Maximum trade size capped by MAX_TRADE_SIZE_USD in .env
Maximum trades per day capped by MAX_TRADES_PER_DAY in .env
ADX below 25 = no trades, regardless of other signals — DaviddTech's #1 rule
Do not trade if RSI(14) is above 70 (overbought) for longs or below 30 (oversold) for shorts
Diversify across pairs — do not concentrate all trades in one asset
Paper trade for minimum 2 weeks before going live — validate forward performance
notes: Extracted from 30 DaviddTech videos. Core philosophy: data over emotion, always backtest before live trading, never trust a single indicator. The ADX filter is his most repeated rule — if the market is choppy, stay out. Strategy designed for 4H timeframe. Scales to 1H. Not suitable for sub-15min timeframes. Indicators used in the bot: EMA(8), EMA(21), MACD, RSI(14), VWAP, ATR — all calculable from raw candle data.
